title: Tablet IX — The Search for Everlasting Life
source: CDLI tablet photograph (P397688) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
description: Tablet IX — The Search for Everlasting Life of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Gilgamesh wanders in grief; journey to the ends of the earth; scorpion-beings at the gate. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P397688). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).
introduction:
This entry presents Tablet IX — The Search for Everlasting Life of the Epic of Gilgamesh — the oldest major work of literature, composed in Akkadian cuneiform around 2100-1200 BCE.

  1. Tablet photograph: Cuneiform tablet photo from CDLI (artifact P397688), showing the actual clay tablet with the wedge-shaped script.
  2. Original text: Akkadian transliteration from Langdon's scholarly edition (public domain).
  3. English translation: Langdon's English rendering (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).
